text: Planning a trip is always exciting, but the real adventure begins when you arrive. Experiencing new cultures can be eye-opening, from different customs to everyday life. One of my favorite parts is trying local food, whether it's street stalls or family-run restaurants. However, language barriers can be tricky—sometimes I end up pointing at menus or using hand gestures. Despite the misunderstandings, these moments become funny memories. Looking back, every challenge taught me something valuable. Travel isn't just about seeing places; it's about the stories you collect and the people you meet along the way.
